title: Wannsee: Quick Summary
items: **Wannsee is still Berlin.** The normal AB fare applies on the S-Bahn, buses and even the F10 passenger ferry. A single ticket covers one journey in one direction for 120 minutes; use another valid ticket for the return or choose a 24-hour ticket for several stops.

**Two stations, two different Wannsees.** S Nikolassee is the beach station, about a 10 minute walk from the Strandbad. S Wannsee is for the ferry pier, bus 114 to the museums and bus 218 to Pfaueninsel.

**Strandbad Wannsee is open daily 9:00 to 20:00 in high summer 2026.** A day ticket is 6.50 EUR (reduced 3.80 EUR); the 12 EUR Badespass ticket covers three people including at least one adult and one child. The 1,275 metre beach opened in 1907.

**Pfaueninsel costs 6 EUR including the island**, with the little ferry normally crossing every 20 minutes, 10:00 to 18:00. Peacocks roam free, and the castle interior is guided tours only, in German, Tuesday to Sunday. SPSG currently lists no ferry on 21, 27, 30 and 31 July 2026.
**The House of the Wannsee Conference is free**, normally open daily 10:00 to 18:00 apart from its listed annual closure dates, reached on bus 114 from S Wannsee. No booking needed for individuals.
**My advice: pick two Wannsees, not four.** Swim last so you ride home dry, and give Pfaueninsel half a day or skip it.
